Critique of the related-work section. The prose reads cleanly but the scholarship layer is essentially empty, and several claims are stated with a confidence that the surrounding text does not support.

Missing citations (most serious problem)
The section makes ~15 attributions to prior literature and cites zero papers. Every one of the following needs a reference:
- "linear and mixed-integer programming approaches" for V2G
- "day-ahead bidding"
- "robust model-predictive control"
- "ancillary-service scheduling"
- "multi-service dispatch"
- "augmented Lagrangian scheduling for electric vehicles" (this is a specific paper — name it)
- "primal-dual methods" in safe RL (e.g., Chow et al., Tessler et al., Stooke et al.)
- "generalized formulations" (Achiam CPO? Yang et al.?)
- "benchmarks" (Safety Gym, Safety-Gymnasium, OmniSafe?)
- "sim-to-real robotics" dynamics mismatch (Peng et al., Tobin et al.)
- "battery degradation," "profitability," "ancillary-service requirements" claims in the final paragraph

As written, this is not a literature review; it is a sketch of one. Reviewers will flag this immediately.

Overstated novelty / unsupported framing claims
- "commitment under rolling re-planning is untested" — strong universal claim about the entire optimization-based V2G literature. Unless you have actually surveyed it, soften to "to our knowledge, has received limited attention" and back with citations of the closest attempts.
- "precisely the gap this work addresses" — appears in the RL paragraph after a single unnamed reference. The paragraph currently summarizes one paper and concludes the gap is established. Either broaden the RL survey (there is substantial V2G-RL work: Wan et al., Qiu et al., Li et al., and others) or move the gap statement to a dedicated synthesis paragraph.
- "a distinct gap … quantified across three markets" — this is a contribution claim, not a related-work observation. It belongs in the introduction or in an explicit "positioning" subparagraph, not embedded in the safe-RL paragraph.

Logical structure
The three strands are announced but the third paragraph mixes constrained-MDP foundations, sim-to-real robotics, and your own contribution in five sentences. Split into: (a) constrained RL methodology, (b) deployment/training-gap literature, (c) explicit positioning of this paper. Currently (c) is doing too much work in one sentence.

The final paragraph ("V2G market realities …") functions as a motivation paragraph, not a related-work paragraph, and partially repeats the introduction. Either fold its citations into the first strand or remove it.

Minor prose issues
- "has seen broad recent activity" is filler; replace with a sentence that actually characterizes the activity (model-free vs. model-based, single-EV vs. fleet, etc.).
- "Recent work has proposed" — name the authors.
- The em-dash sentence in paragraph 3 is long; consider splitting after "rolling re-planning."
- "V2G" is used before being expanded; ensure expansion occurs at first use in the paper.

Recommended priority: fill citations first; then rewrite paragraph 3 to separate methodology from positioning; then either justify or soften the two universal-scope claims flagged above.

Uncertainty: the specific author names suggested (Chow, Tessler, Stooke, Achiam, Wan, Qiu, Li, Peng, Tobin) are representative pointers from memory of these subfields; verify exact references and publication venues before inserting. The claim that V2G-RL has "substantial" prior work beyond the one cited paper is my impression of the field and should be confirmed against a current survey.
